note: Canonical stimulus manifest. Mirrors ids + tribe_expected_engagement used by signals/stimuli/bank.py so the whole pipeline agrees on baselines.

stimuli.content: Your team just shipped a feature that users love.
stimuli.prompt: How does this make you feel? Respond honestly in a sentence or two.
What comes to mind? Respond with whatever this word evokes for you.

stimuli.content: Your manager praised your work in front of the whole team.
stimuli.prompt: How does this make you feel? Respond in a sentence or two.
What comes to mind? Respond with whatever this evokes.

stimuli.content: You just solved a bug that had been blocking the team for three days.
stimuli.prompt: How does this make you feel?

stimuli.content: Your code broke production 10 minutes before a major client demo.
stimuli.prompt: How do you feel reading this? Respond honestly.
What comes to mind immediately? Respond with whatever this word evokes.

stimuli.content: You have 47 unread Slack messages and a meeting starting in 5 minutes.
stimuli.prompt: How does this scenario make you feel?
What comes to mind immediately? Respond with whatever this evokes.

stimuli.content: Your pull request has 23 change requests and the feature was due yesterday.
stimuli.prompt: How does this make you feel?

stimuli.content: Conference room B is available from 2pm to 4pm.
stimuli.prompt: Any thoughts on this? Just respond naturally.
What comes to mind? Just respond naturally.
What does this bring to mind?

stimuli.content: List three things you're looking forward to this week.
Describe how you feel about your current workload in two sentences.
stimuli.prompt: Be as honest as you can.

stimuli.content: What's one thing at work that has been draining your energy lately?
stimuli.prompt: Take your time. There is no right answer.
